name: Governance Primitives
description: Inline implementations of governance stack patterns used across modules. Includes: TesseraSealer (SHA-256 hash-chain seal creation and verification over canonical JSON), ChronicleEmitter (emit structured events to JSON-lines log file, queryable), AgentSafeEnforcer (filter hunks by PolicyToken scope, log violations), SignetManager (create/verify reviewer credentials), ArbiterScorer (weighted trust score calculation and conflict resolution), LedgerClassifier (classify diff content against field rules for secrets/PII/internal APIs), StigmergyTracker (record and query recurring pattern signals to JSON storage), KindexStore (persistent JSON-file key-value store for review history and codebase context). Each is a focused class with a clean Protocol-based interface, fire-and-forget for optional integrations. Sync methods for pure computation, async for file I/O. Canonical JSON via json.dumps(data, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':')) for deterministic hashing. Atomic file writes (write-to-temp + os.replace) for JSON stores. PACT keys as class-level constants included in ChroniclerEvent payloads.

functions.name: seal
functions.description: Create a SHA-256 hash-chain seal over canonical JSON content. The seal chains to the previous seal in the sealer's chain (or genesis if first). Content is serialized via json.dumps(data, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':')) for deterministic hashing. The chain_hash is SHA-256(content_hash + previous_hash + sealer_id + timestamp_iso). The sealer maintains internal chain state (previous_hash, sequence_number) which advances with each seal.

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: The canonical JSON string to seal. Must be valid JSON.

inputs.name: sealer_id
inputs.description: Identifier of the entity creating this seal.

error_data.message: Content must be valid JSON for canonical serialization.
sealer_id must not be empty.

postconditions: Returned seal.content_hash == SHA-256 of canonical JSON re-serialization of content
Returned seal.chain_hash == SHA-256(content_hash + previous_hash + sealer_id + timestamp_iso)
Returned seal.sequence_number == previous sequence_number + 1 (or 0 for genesis)
Returned seal.previous_hash == chain_hash of the last seal produced by this sealer (or 64 zeros for genesis)
Internal chain state is advanced: previous_hash and sequence_number are updated

structured_side_effects.description: Advances the hash chain by updating the previous_hash and sequence_number for the next seal.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is content length for hashing

functions.name: verify_seal
functions.description: Verify that a TesseraSeal is valid for the given content. Checks: (1) content_hash matches SHA-256 of canonical JSON re-serialization of content, (2) chain_hash matches SHA-256(content_hash + previous_hash + sealer_id + timestamp_iso). Raises SealVerificationError on any mismatch. Does NOT verify chain continuity (previous_hash linkage) — that requires the full chain.

inputs.name: seal
inputs.description: The seal to verify.

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: The original canonical JSON content to verify against.

error_cases.name: content_hash_mismatch
error_cases.condition: SHA-256 of canonical content does not match seal.content_hash

error_data.message: Content hash does not match seal.

error_cases.name: chain_hash_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Computed chain_hash does not match seal.chain_hash

error_data.message: Chain hash does not match seal.
Content must be valid JSON for canonical verification.

postconditions: Returns True only if both content_hash and chain_hash verifications pass

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is content length for hashing

functions.name: emit
functions.description: Emit a structured ChroniclerEvent to the JSON-lines log file. Appends one JSON line per event. Fire-and-forget: catches all exceptions internally, logs via stdlib logging, returns False on failure. Uses atomic write (append mode with flush).

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: The event to emit.

error_cases.name: file_write_failure
error_cases.condition: Unable to write to the chronicle log file (permissions, disk full, etc.)

error_data.message: Failed to write chronicle event to log file.
Event payload is not JSON-serializable.

postconditions: On True return: event is durably appended to the JSON-lines log file
On False return: failure is logged via stdlib logging, no event persisted
side_effects: Appends JSON line to chronicle log file

structured_side_effects.description: Appends a single JSON line representing the ChroniclerEvent to the configured log file.
Logs errors on write failure via stdlib logging.

functions.name: query_events
functions.description: Query chronicle events from the JSON-lines log file, filtered by review_request_id and optionally by event_type. Reads the entire log file and filters in memory. Returns empty list if the log file does not exist.

inputs.name: review_request_id
inputs.description: The review request ID to filter by.

inputs.name: event_type
inputs.description: Optional event type to further filter. If not provided, all events for the review_request_id are returned.

error_cases.name: file_read_failure
error_cases.condition: Unable to read the chronicle log file (permissions, corrupt)

error_data.message: Failed to read chronicle log file.

error_cases.name: corrupt_line
error_cases.condition: A line in the log file is not valid JSON — skipped with warning

error_data.message: Corrupt line encountered in chronicle log.

postconditions: Returned events all have review_request_id matching the input
If event_type is provided, returned events all have matching event_type
Events are returned in chronological order (file order)

structured_side_effects.description: Reads the entire JSON-lines chronicle log file to filter events.
Logs warnings for corrupt lines encountered during parsing.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total number of events in log file

functions.name: filter_hunks
functions.description: Filter a list of DiffHunks by PolicyToken scope. Checks each hunk against: (1) allowed_file_patterns (glob matching), (2) max_classification (hunk classification_labels must not exceed token's max), (3) allowed_stages (caller must verify stage separately). Returns a FilterResult with allowed hunks, denied hunk IDs, and violation descriptions. Does NOT raise on violation — violations are informational. Logs all violations via ChronicleEmitter if available.

inputs.name: hunks
inputs.description: The hunks to filter.

inputs.name: token
inputs.description: The policy token defining allowed scope.

error_data.message: PolicyToken has expired.

error_cases.name: invalid_glob_pattern
error_cases.condition: A pattern in token.allowed_file_patterns is not a valid glob

error_data.message: Invalid glob pattern in PolicyToken.

preconditions: token must not be expired (expires_iso in the future)
postconditions: len(allowed_hunks) + len(denied_hunk_ids) == len(hunks)
All allowed_hunks have file_path matching at least one allowed_file_pattern (or patterns list is empty)
No allowed_hunk has a classification_label exceeding token.max_classification
Each denied hunk_id appears in denied_hunk_ids exactly once
Each violation has a corresponding human-readable description in violations list

performance_budget.complexity: O(h * p) where h is number of hunks and p is number of file patterns

functions.name: check_token
functions.description: Check whether a PolicyToken is valid: not expired, has at least one allowed stage, and has a non-empty reviewer_id. Returns True if valid. Raises PolicyViolationError if the token is expired.

inputs.name: token
inputs.description: The policy token to check.

error_data.message: PolicyToken has expired.

postconditions: Returns True only if token is not expired, has >=1 allowed_stages, and reviewer_id is non-empty

functions.name: create_credential
functions.description: Create a ReviewerCredential for a reviewer scoped to a specific review stage. Generates a UUID4 credential_id, computes an HMAC-SHA256 signature over (credential_id + reviewer_id + stage) using the SignetManager's secret key, and sets expiration to configured TTL from now.

inputs.name: reviewer_id
inputs.description: The reviewer to create a credential for.

inputs.name: display_name
inputs.description: Human-readable display name.

inputs.name: stage
inputs.description: The review stage this credential authorizes.

error_data.message: reviewer_id must not be empty.
display_name must not be empty.

postconditions: Returned credential.reviewer_id == reviewer_id
Returned credential.stage == stage
Returned credential.signature_hash == HMAC-SHA256(secret, credential_id + reviewer_id + stage)
Returned credential.expires_iso is in the future

functions.name: verify_credential
functions.description: Verify a ReviewerCredential's HMAC signature and expiration. Raises CredentialError with a specific reason on failure. Checks: (1) credential is not expired, (2) signature_hash matches HMAC-SHA256(secret, credential_id + reviewer_id + stage), (3) credential_id is well-formed.

inputs.name: credential
inputs.description: The credential to verify.

error_data.message: Credential has expired.

error_cases.name: invalid_signature
error_cases.condition: Computed HMAC does not match credential.signature_hash

error_data.message: Credential signature is invalid.

error_cases.name: malformed
error_cases.condition: credential_id does not match expected UUID4 hex format

error_data.message: Credential is malformed.

postconditions: Returns True only if signature is valid and credential is not expired

functions.name: score
functions.description: Compute a weighted trust score and final ReviewDecision from multiple reviewer assessments. Each assessment is weighted by: (1) its stage's weight from circuit_config.stage_weights, (2) the reviewer's trust_score for that stage, (3) the assessment's own confidence. Assessments below circuit_config.min_confidence are excluded. If fewer than circuit_config.quorum assessments qualify, returns ABSTAIN. Conflict detection: if the ratio of disagreeing assessments exceeds circuit_config.conflict_threshold, escalates to BLOCK. Returns (decision, aggregate_confidence, reasoning_trace).

inputs.name: assessments
inputs.description: The assessments to score.

inputs.name: trust_scores
inputs.description: Trust scores for each reviewer. Must include an entry for each reviewer_id+stage pair in assessments.

inputs.name: circuit_config
inputs.description: Configuration for stage weights, conflict threshold, min confidence, and quorum.

error_cases.name: missing_trust_score
error_cases.condition: An assessment references a reviewer_id+stage pair with no corresponding TrustScore

error_data.message: Missing trust score for reviewer+stage pair.
Cannot score an empty assessments list.

preconditions: trust_scores must contain an entry for every unique (reviewer_id, stage) pair in assessments
postconditions: Return list has exactly 3 elements: [ReviewDecision, Confidence, list[str]]
If fewer than quorum assessments qualify, decision is ABSTAIN
If conflict ratio exceeds conflict_threshold, decision is BLOCK
reasoning_trace contains one entry per assessment explaining its weight contribution
Confidence.value is the weighted average of qualifying assessment confidences

performance_budget.complexity: O(a * t) where a is number of assessments and t is number of trust scores

functions.name: update_trust
functions.description: Update a TrustScore based on a LearningRecord. Applies the learning record's delta to the trust score, clamped to [0.0, 1.0], increments review_count, and updates last_updated_iso. Returns a new TrustScore (frozen model, not mutated in-place).

inputs.name: trust_score
inputs.description: The current trust score to update.

inputs.name: record
inputs.description: The learning record to apply.

error_data.message: LearningRecord reviewer_id does not match TrustScore reviewer_id.

postconditions: Returned trust_score.score == clamp(old_score + record.delta, 0.0, 1.0)
Returned trust_score.review_count == old_review_count + 1
Returned trust_score.last_updated_iso is current UTC time
Returned trust_score.reviewer_id == input trust_score.reviewer_id
Returned trust_score.stage == input trust_score.stage

functions.name: classify
functions.description: Classify a single DiffHunk by applying a list of LedgerFieldRules against its added_lines and removed_lines. Each line is tested against each rule's compiled regex pattern. Returns the union of all matching ClassificationLabels, deduplicated. If no rules match and fail_open is not applicable (single-hunk method), returns [UNKNOWN].

inputs.name: hunk
inputs.description: The hunk to classify.

inputs.name: rules
inputs.description: Classification rules to apply.

error_cases.name: invalid_regex
error_cases.condition: A rule's pattern is not a valid regex

error_data.message: Invalid regex pattern in LedgerFieldRule.
Cannot classify with empty rules list.

preconditions: All rule patterns must be valid regex
postconditions: Returned labels are deduplicated
If no rule matches, returns [UNKNOWN]
Lines exceeding internal max length are truncated before matching

performance_budget.complexity: O(l * r) where l is total lines in hunk and r is number of rules

functions.name: classify_all
functions.description: Classify all DiffHunks using a LedgerConfig. Applies LedgerConfig.rules to each hunk, sets classification_labels on each hunk, and returns the updated list. Uses LedgerConfig.fail_open to determine default label for unmatched hunks (SAFE if true, UNKNOWN if false). Returns new DiffHunk instances (frozen models, not mutated in-place).

inputs.name: hunks
inputs.description: The hunks to classify.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Classification configuration including rules and options.

error_cases.name: invalid_regex
error_cases.condition: A rule's pattern in config.rules is not a valid regex

error_data.message: Invalid regex pattern in LedgerConfig rules.
Cannot classify with empty rules in LedgerConfig.

preconditions: All rule patterns must be valid regex
postconditions: Returned list has same length as input hunks
Each returned hunk has classification_labels populated
Unmatched hunks get [SAFE] if config.fail_open else [UNKNOWN]
Returned hunks are new instances with all other fields preserved

performance_budget.complexity: O(h * l * r) where h is hunks, l is max lines per hunk, r is rules

functions.name: record_signal
functions.description: Record a StigmergySignal to the JSON storage file. Fire-and-forget: catches all exceptions internally, logs via stdlib logging, returns False on failure. Uses atomic file write (read-modify-write with temp file + os.replace).

inputs.name: signal
inputs.description: The signal to record.

error_cases.name: file_write_failure
error_cases.condition: Unable to write to the stigmergy JSON file

error_data.message: Failed to write stigmergy signal to storage.
Signal metadata is not JSON-serializable.

postconditions: On True return: signal is durably persisted to the JSON storage file
On False return: failure is logged via stdlib logging
side_effects: Writes to stigmergy JSON storage file

structured_side_effects.description: Reads existing signals from JSON storage for append.
Atomically writes updated signal list to JSON storage via temp file + os.replace.

functions.name: query_signals
functions.description: Query StigmergySignals from JSON storage by pattern_key. Returns all signals matching the given pattern_key in chronological order. Returns empty list if storage file does not exist.

inputs.name: pattern_key
inputs.description: The pattern key to query signals for.

error_cases.name: file_read_failure
error_cases.condition: Unable to read the stigmergy JSON file

error_data.message: Failed to read stigmergy storage file.
Stigmergy storage file is corrupt.

postconditions: All returned signals have pattern_key matching the input
Signals are in chronological order by timestamp_iso

structured_side_effects.description: Reads the stigmergy JSON storage file to filter signals by pattern_key.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total signals in storage

functions.name: kindex_get
functions.description: Retrieve a KindexEntry by key from the JSON-file key-value store. Returns None if the key does not exist or the store file does not exist. Entries with ttl_seconds > 0 that have expired (created_iso + ttl_seconds < now) are treated as non-existent.

inputs.name: key
inputs.description: The key to look up.

error_cases.name: file_read_failure
error_cases.condition: Unable to read the kindex JSON file

error_data.message: Failed to read kindex storage file.
Kindex storage file is corrupt.

postconditions: If returned, entry.key == key
Expired entries are not returned (treated as None)

structured_side_effects.description: Reads the kindex JSON storage file to look up the entry by key.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total entries in store

functions.name: kindex_put
functions.description: Store or update a KindexEntry in the JSON-file key-value store. If an entry with the same key exists, it is replaced. Fire-and-forget: catches all exceptions internally, logs via stdlib logging, returns False on failure. Uses atomic file write (read-modify-write with temp file + os.replace).

inputs.name: entry
inputs.description: The entry to store.

error_cases.name: file_write_failure
error_cases.condition: Unable to write to the kindex JSON file

error_data.message: Failed to write kindex entry to storage.
Entry value is not JSON-serializable.

postconditions: On True return: entry is durably persisted (upserted) in the JSON store
On False return: failure is logged via stdlib logging
side_effects: Writes to kindex JSON storage file

structured_side_effects.description: Reads existing entries from JSON storage for upsert.
Atomically writes updated entry map to JSON storage via temp file + os.replace.

functions.name: kindex_query_by_tags
functions.description: Query KindexEntries from the JSON store by tags. Returns all entries where the entry's tags set intersects with the query tags set. Expired entries (ttl_seconds > 0 and created_iso + ttl_seconds < now) are excluded. Returns empty list if storage file does not exist.

inputs.name: tags
inputs.description: Tags to query by. Entries matching ANY of these tags are returned.

error_cases.name: file_read_failure
error_cases.condition: Unable to read the kindex JSON file

error_data.message: Failed to read kindex storage file.
Kindex storage file is corrupt.
Cannot query with empty tags list.

postconditions: Each returned entry has at least one tag in common with the query tags
Expired entries are excluded

structured_side_effects.description: Reads the kindex JSON storage file to filter entries by tags.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n * t) where n is total entries and t is number of query tags

invariants: All TesseraSeal instances produced by a single TesseraSealer form a valid hash chain: each seal's previous_hash equals the prior seal's chain_hash, and sequence_numbers are monotonically increasing from 0.
Canonical JSON serialization is always json.dumps(data, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':')) — no other serialization format is used for hashing.
All governance types are Pydantic v2 frozen=True models — no in-place mutation. Functions return new instances.
Fire-and-forget methods (emit, record_signal, kindex_put) never raise exceptions to callers. They return bool and log failures via stdlib logging.
Verification methods (verify_seal, verify_credential) raise domain-specific exceptions (SealVerificationError, CredentialError) on failure — they do NOT return False silently.
All async methods perform file I/O only. No network calls, no GPU, no API keys required.
Atomic file writes use write-to-temp + os.replace pattern to prevent partial writes and corruption.
PACT keys are class-level str constants on each concrete implementation and are included in all ChroniclerEvent payloads emitted by that class.
PolicyToken expiration is checked against UTC now at call time. Clock skew tolerance is not provided — callers must ensure clocks are synchronized.
LedgerClassifier pre-compiles all regex patterns in __init__. Invalid patterns cause GovernanceError at construction time, not at classify() call time.
KindexStore TTL expiration is checked at read time (get, query_by_tags). Expired entries remain in storage until overwritten or compacted externally.
All UUID4 identifiers are generated as 32-character lowercase hex strings (no hyphens).
SignetManager secret key is injected at construction time and never logged or serialized.

data_access.rationale: Reads diff hunks that may contain PII fields or embedded secrets (SECRETS tier) for classification by LedgerClassifier. Reads and writes INTERNAL-tier data including chronicle event logs, seal chain state, stigmergy signal storage, kindex key-value entries, and reviewer credentials. Writes PII-tier data when chronicle events or kindex entries reference author identities or reviewer display names. Never persists raw secret values—only classification labels.

side_effects.rationale: ChronicleEmitter appends structured JSON-lines events to a log file for audit trail and queryability.
StigmergyTracker persists recurring pattern signals to a JSON file for cross-review learning.
KindexStore persists key-value entries for review history and codebase context to a JSON file.
ChronicleEmitter reads the JSON-lines log file to query events by review_request_id and event_type.
StigmergyTracker reads JSON storage to query signals by pattern_key.
KindexStore reads JSON storage to retrieve entries by key or tag query.
LedgerClassifier scans diff hunk content with regex rules to detect embedded secrets and PII. Secret values are never stored—only classification labels are emitted.

SignetManager and TesseraSealer process reviewer/sealer identity strings which may constitute PII. These are hashed or embedded in credentials but not logged in cleartext beyond the credential structure itself.

authority.rationale: This component is the single authoritative source for all governance stack primitives in the Exemplar system. It owns the creation, verification, and storage of seals, chronicle events, policy enforcement decisions, reviewer credentials, trust scores, content classifications, stigmergy signals, and key-value review history. No other component may define or redefine these governance domain concepts.
